reasoning: An early federal election took place on April 28, 2025, in which Mark Carney led the Liberal Party to a minority government victory, winning 169 out of 343 seats (just 3 short of a majority). Because the Liberals only need the support of any single other party (like the NDP or the Bloc Québécois, whose leader stated they expect to collaborate for at least a year), the government is highly stable. With only four months remaining until the highly stringent January 1, 2026 resolution date, there is practically zero chance of the government falling, a new snap election being called, and Poilievre winning and taking office before 2026. The true probability is extremely close to 0%, but the platform limit is 0.02.
